title: Error: OCI runtime error: error executing hook /usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook(exit code: 1)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Constantly getting an error `Error: OCI runtime error: error executing hook `/usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook` (exit code: 1)
`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

I am working on porting the [script](https://github.com/rust-serverless/lambda-rust/blob/master/tests/test.sh) to the Podman.
The failing command is [here](https://github.com/rust-serverless/lambda-rust/blob/master/tests/test.sh#L96) (just replace it with Podman). So for me the failing command is: `podman run -i -e DOCKER_LAMBDA_USE_STDIN=1 --rm -v -v /tmp/lambda:/var/task:Z docker.io/lambci/lambda:provided.al2`

**Describe the results you received:**
Just one error:
`Error: OCI runtime error: error executing hook `/usr/libexec/oci/hooks.d/oci-systemd-hook` (exit code: 1)`

**Describe the results you expected:**
The container shall start successfully.

Invoking with `--log-level=trace` gives the following output:


comments.body: Podman does not require this hook (we handle systemd-in-container natively) so I recommend you uninstall the hook.

@mheon I am just confused a little bit since I don't know, what exactly the hook mechanism is. Can you explain me (or provide corresponding links) please? Also, I am not sure that I should delete such hooks since seems like that they are installed with `podman` package - maybe it just should be deleted by `podman` maintainers in Fedora.
Remove that hook it is not necessary with Podman. Or CRI-O

An OCI hook is an extension mechanism for container runtimes that allows for additional functionality not implemented in Docker or Podman to be added. An example of this is are the Nvidia container tools, which use an OCI hook to modify the container to make a GPU available inside it. `oci-systemd-hook` was an old one of these used to add support for systemd in a container, but it is not required by Podman as we have added native support for this feature.

I guarantee that it is not installed by or required by the `podman` package. Removing it should remove the problem.
Thanks for the explanation! I've removed a package that provided this hook and now it works. Thanks for the help. I think it can be closed as "Resolved".
